title: quercus × trabutii
intro: Quercus × trabutii is a rare hybrid species between Quercus subpyramidalis and Quercus pinnatiloba, resulting from the natural cross-pollination of these two oak tree species. The bark and leaves of the Quercus × trabutii plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly for their astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Quercus × trabutii include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the bark and leaves of Quercus × trabutii are often infused in hot water to make a tea, or dried to make a powder that can be added to capsules or other formulations.
